title: Progressivism
description: What was going on in America between the presidencies of Theodore Roosevelt and Woodrow Wilson?

assessment_items.question_content:
What are two issues **Theodore Roosevelt** focused on during this presidency?




[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Great Depression did not take place during Theodore Roosevelt’s presidency.

choices.content: Protections for workers and strong foreign policy
Strengthening the bargaining position of trusts and protecting American isolationism
Fighting the Great Depression and keeping the United States out of World War II

assessment_items.question_content:
**Upton Sinclair**, a journalist who exposed the poor conditions and corruption of Chicago’s meatpacking industry in his novel _The Jungle_, is an example of a



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: A suffragist was someone who campaigned to secure a woman’s right to vote.

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Meat Inspection Act** and the **Pure Food and Drug Act**, which gave the federal government regulatory power over food and drugs, were enacted largely as a result of



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Meat Inspection and Pure Food and Drug Acts were not the result of industry requests.

choices.content: public outcry following Upton Sinclair’s exposé novel, _The Jungle_, which highlighted food contamination issues.
sustained lobbying from Progressive women’s groups organized by influential female leaders such as Jane Addams and Ida B. Wells
requests from large meatpacking and canning businesses, who hoped to drive smaller competitors out of business

assessment_items.question_content:
**Jacob Riis,** a muckraker photographer, produced a photo essay called _How the Other Half Lives: Studies Among the Tenements of New York_, which led to





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: _How the Other Half Lives_ didn’t lead to an increase in sweatshops in Manhattan.

choices.content: greater visibility of living conditions of the poor and led to housing reforms addressing space, cleanliness, and safety.

one of the first immigration laws in the United States, which starting in 1906 reduced the number of immigrants from Southern and Eastern Europe permitted to enter the country to just a few thousand per year.
an increase in the number of sweatshops in the Lower East Side of Manhattan as textile factories discovered a new source of cheap labor in the form of immigrants.

assessment_items.question_content:
The main motive behind **“yellow journalism”** was to

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: William Randolph Hearst, king of the yellow journalists, agitated for the United States to make war with Spain over the horrific conditions of Cuba, catalogued in his newspaper.

choices.content: sell newspapers by telling sensationalist stories.
acquaint readers with the serious environmental issues facing the United States
promote Progressive legislation by confronting readers with disgusting living and working conditions
